industries.name: Flame Retardants
uses: Antimony trioxide (Sb₂O₃) is used exclusively as a synergist with halogenated flame retardants — it does not retard flame alone but reacts with bromine or chlorine released during combustion to form antimony oxyhalide and antimony trihalide gases that interrupt the combustion chain reaction

Sb₂O₃ + brominated flame retardants in ABS, HIPS, and polypropylene for TV housings, computer cases, electrical enclosures, and automotive components — the combination is far more effective than either ingredient alone
Flame-retardant textiles for upholstered furniture, mattresses, and children's sleepwear incorporate Sb₂O₃ with decabromodiphenyl ether or alternative brominated compounds
Aircraft seat fabrics, carpet underlays, and overhead bin liners use Sb₂O₃-halogen FR systems meeting FAA and EASA aviation flammability requirements

companies.name: Hunan Gold Corporation (Xikuangshan)
companies.use: China dominates world antimony production — ~55% of global supply. The Xikuangshan antimony mine in Hunan province is the world's largest antimony deposit. Produces antimony trioxide and antimony metal for flame retardant and lead-acid battery markets.

companies.name: Campine
companies.use: Major European antimony trioxide producer and lead recycler. Produces Sb₂O₃ from antimony-bearing lead alloys recovered from recycled lead-acid batteries. Supplies flame retardant synergist to plastics and textile manufacturers in Europe.

companies.name: Lanxess
companies.use: Major flame retardant chemical supplier. Produces and formulates Sb₂O₃-brominated FR masterbatch systems for ABS, HIPS, and polyolefin applications in electronics and automotive markets.

industries.name: Lead-Acid Batteries
uses: Antimony (2–6%) alloyed with lead in battery grid alloys: hardens the soft lead grid, improves castability, and reduces creep under load — critical for deep-cycle and traction (forklift) lead-acid batteries

Low-antimony alloys (1–2% Sb) for starting-lighting-ignition (SLI) automotive batteries — antimony reduces water loss and gassing, but calcium-lead alloys have largely replaced antimony in maintenance-free automotive batteries

Antimony migration from positive to negative plates in flooded lead-acid batteries causes self-discharge — driving the shift to calcium-lead alloys in automotive batteries while antimony remains in deep-cycle applications
Recycled antimony from secondary lead smelters: antimony is recovered during lead-acid battery recycling, providing a significant secondary source that supplies ~30% of antimony demand

companies.name: EnerSys
companies.use: Major deep-cycle and industrial lead-acid battery manufacturer. Uses antimony-lead alloy grids in its deep-cycle batteries for golf carts, floor scrubbers, and standby power applications where deep discharge cycle life is paramount.

companies.name: Exide Technologies
companies.use: Major automotive and industrial lead-acid battery manufacturer. Uses antimony-lead alloys in deep-cycle and traction batteries for forklifts, golf carts, and stationary UPS applications.

industries.name: Glass, Ceramics & Specialty
uses: Antimony trioxide as glass fining agent — removes bubbles from molten optical and specialty glass by oxidizing at high temperature, releasing oxygen to absorb remaining dissolved gas; used in CRT glass, optical glass, and specialty glass where clarity is critical

Antimony sulfide (Sb₂S₃) in friction materials (brake pads and linings) as a friction modifier and lubricant — reduces brake squeal and maintains consistent friction coefficient across temperature range
Antimony-doped tin oxide (ATO) as a transparent conducting oxide and UV-absorbing coating on glass and plastics — used in anti-static coatings, IR-reflecting window films, and energy-saving glass
Sodium antimonate (NaSbO₃) as a white opacifier in ceramic glazes — competes with zirconium silicate for tile opacification in markets where antimony is locally available

companies.name: Akebono Brake Industry
companies.use: Major automotive brake pad manufacturer. Uses antimony sulfide as a friction modifier in its semi-metallic and non-asbestos organic (NAO) brake formulations for passenger car and truck brake systems.

companies.name: Federal-Mogul (Tenneco)
companies.use: Major brake lining and friction material manufacturer using antimony sulfide in friction formulations for automotive disc pads and drum brake linings. Supplies to OEM and aftermarket channels globally.
